engineGuide.h2: Honda Integra Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Honda Integra across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: First DOHC VTEC in Integra; 158hp, 8,200rpm redline defined the DA6 era.
compatibleVariants: DA6 XSi, DA6 RSi, DA8 XSi
commonFailures: Distributor failure on OBD0/OBD1 units (60k-100k miles)
Timing belt wear if not replaced at 60k mile intervals
Valve seal wear causing oil consumption on high-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for B16A Honda Integra engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: Legendary Type R heart; 190-200ps, hand-built, the most sought-after Integra engine.
compatibleVariants: DC2 Type R (96 spec, 98 spec), DB8 Type R, DC2 Type Rx, DC2 SiR-G
commonFailures: VTEC solenoid failure causing no VTEC engagement (40k-80k miles)
Oil consumption from worn valve seals or piston rings (80k+ miles)
Cam seal leaks around distributor during timing belt service
entries.cta: Get quotes for B18C Honda Integra engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: UKDM DC2 Type R variant; 190bhp, OBD2, lower compression than JDM B18C.
commonFailures: VTEC solenoid failure (40k-80k miles)
Idle air control valve causing hunting idle
Oil consumption on high-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for B18C6 Honda Integra engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: USDM GS-R engine; 170bhp VTEC, popular budget alternative to Type R units.
commonFailures: VTEC solenoid failure
Timing belt tensioner wear
Idle surge from IACV carbon buildup
entries.cta: Get quotes for B18C1 Honda Integra engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: From MB6 Civic/MG ZS; same architecture as B18C, popular cheaper replacement option.
commonFailures: VTEC solenoid failure
Oil consumption at high mileage
Idle air control valve issues
entries.cta: Get quotes for B18C4 Honda Integra engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: Non-VTEC workhorse; 142bhp, reliable but less exciting, common in LS/RS models.
compatibleVariants: DC2 LS, DC2 RS, DC2 SE
commonFailures: Valve seal wear causing oil consumption (70k+ miles)
Timing belt failure if neglected (catastrophic)
Distributor failure on early OBD1 units
entries.cta: Get quotes for B18B Honda Integra engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: DC5 Type R flagship; 217bhp i-VTEC, 6-speed, the ultimate naturally aspirated Integra engine.
commonFailures: VTC actuator rattle on cold start (30k-60k miles)
Timing chain tensioner wear causing chain rattle
Oil consumption on high-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for K20A Honda Integra engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: EP3 Civic Type R unit; often swapped into DC2 chassis for K-swap builds.
compatibleVariants: DC2 K-swap builds, DC5 Type R (early)
commonFailures: VTC actuator noise on startup
Oil consumption at high mileage
Timing chain tensioner wear

commonProblems.h2: Honda Integra Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Honda Integra's VTEC engines are renowned for performance, but B-series timing belts, VTEC solenoids, and high-mileage oil consumption drive most replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: B18C VTEC Solenoid Failure - Why Type R Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Honda Integra DC2 Type R, SiR-G, GS-R (1994-2001, B18C, B18C1, B18C4)
problems.rootCause: Oil pressure screen clogging or solenoid electrical failure prevents VTEC engagement.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace VTEC solenoid and clean oil screen
repairOptions.longevity: Restores VTEC; preventative if caught early

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace solenoid, pressure switch, flush oil system
repairOptions.longevity: More comprehensive fix for persistent VTEC codes

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded VTEC components

problems.recommendation: If VTEC failure is caught early and the engine runs smoothly otherwise, solenoid replacement offers excellent value. However, if the engine has high mileage or shows oil consumption, investing in a rebuilt B18C with upgraded components is the smarter long-term choice for a Type R.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt B18C engine prices for your Honda Integra

problems.h4: B-Series Timing Belt Failure - Catastrophic Risk on Integra Engines
problems.affectedModels: Honda Integra DA6/DC2 with B16A, B18C, B18B (1989-2001)
problems.rootCause: Timing belt wears or snaps; B-series are interference engines so valve damage follows.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, water pump

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Valve Repair After Belt Snap
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head rebuild, valve replacement, timing service
Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new timing components

problems.recommendation: Never skip the 60,000-mile timing belt interval on B-series Integras. If your belt has snapped, a rebuilt engine is often more cost-effective than cylinder head repair and gives you a fresh start with warranty protection.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Honda Integra B-series engine replacement

problems.h4: B18C Oil Consumption - High-Mileage Valve Seal & Ring Wear
problems.affectedModels: Honda Integra DC2 Type R, GS-R, SiR-G (1994-2001, B18C variants)
problems.rootCause: Worn valve stem seals and/or piston rings allow oil into combustion chamber.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace valve stem seals via cylinder head removal
repairOptions.longevity: Reduces oil burn; doesn't address ring wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine strip, new rings, honing, reassemble
Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded seals/rings
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage Type R owners

problems.recommendation: For a genuine Type R worth preserving, a rebuilt B18C with upgraded internal components is the most reliable and cost-effective solution versus repeated top-end repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt B18C engine prices for your Honda Integra

problems.h4: B16A Distributor Failure - No Spark on Early DA6 Integras
problems.affectedModels: Honda Integra DA6 XSi, RSi (1989-1993, B16A, B16A1, B16A2)
problems.rootCause: Internal distributor coil or igniter failure common on OBD0/OBD1 B16A engines.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace distributor assembly with OEM or upgraded unit
repairOptions.longevity: Restores spark; preventative if symptoms appear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Convert to modern coil-on-plug ignition system
repairOptions.longevity: More reliable long-term; requires wiring mods

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with updated ignition
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage or track-use DA6 Integras

problems.recommendation: If your DA6 Integra is a daily driver, distributor replacement is cost-effective. For track or performance builds, consider a rebuilt B16A or coil-on-plug conversion for reliability.
problems.cta: Check rebuilt B16A engine availability for your Honda Integra

problems.h4: K20A VTC Actuator Rattle - Cold-Start Noise on DC5 Type R
problems.affectedModels: Honda Integra DC5 Type R (2001-2006, K20A, K20Z1)
problems.rootCause: VTC actuator gear wear causes rattling noise on cold start; can affect cam timing.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace VTC actuator gear on intake cam
repairOptions.longevity: Eliminates rattle; preventative if noise is mild

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace VTC actuator, chain, tensioner, guides
Supply & fit rebuilt K20A with updated VTC components
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage DC5 Type R preservation

problems.recommendation: If the rattle is mild and the engine runs well, actuator replacement is sufficient. For DC5 Type R owners planning long-term ownership, a rebuilt K20A with upgraded components offers peace of mind.
problems.cta: Get quotes for K20A Honda Integra engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Honda Integra
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're dealing with VTEC solenoid issues, timing belt concerns, or high-mileage oil consumption, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Honda Integra specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices and get your Integra back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Honda Integra Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Honda Integra and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.title: Honda Integra 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L B16A powered the first-generation DA6/DA8 Integra (1989-1993), introducing DOHC VTEC to the model with a thrilling 8,200rpm redline and 158bhp output.
commonFailurePoints: Distributor failure on OBD0/OBD1 units; timing belt wear if neglected

items.title: Honda Integra 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8L B18 series defined the DC2 era (1994-2001), with the VTEC B18C powering the legendary Type R and the non-VTEC B18B serving LS/RS models as a reliable workhorse.
commonFailurePoints: VTEC solenoid failure on B18C variants; oil consumption on high-mileage examples
items.cta: Compare Honda Integra 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.description: The 2.0L K20A i-VTEC powered the final DC5 Integra Type R (2001-2006), delivering 217bhp with advanced variable valve timing and a 6-speed gearbox. Not sure which engine size your Honda Integra needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. fuelTypes.h2: Honda Integra Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Honda Integra engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early B16A VTEC to the latest K20A i-VTEC.

items.title: Honda Integra Petrol Engines
items.description: All Honda Integra models sold in the UK market were petrol-powered, spanning B-series VTEC (1989-2001) and K-series i-VTEC (2001-2006) engines.
items.descriptor: All Honda Integra models sold in the UK market were petrol-powered, spanning B-series VTEC (1989-2001) and K-series i-VTEC (2001-2006) engines.
items.familiesLabel: Common Honda Integra Petrol Engine Families
families: B16A - 1.6L VTEC from DA6/DA8 (1989-1993), 158bhp, distributor failure common
B18C - 1.8L VTEC from DC2/DB8 Type R (1996-2001), 190-200ps, VTEC solenoid issues
B18B - 1.8L non-VTEC from DC2 LS/RS (1994-2001), 142bhp, reliable workhorse
K20A - 2.0L i-VTEC from DC5 Type R (2001-2006), 217bhp, VTC actuator rattle
knownFor: High-revving VTEC engagement above 5,800rpm on B18C/B16A
Timing BELT service required every 60,000 miles on B-series engines
Strong enthusiast community and aftermarket support for all variants
Oil consumption common on high-mileage B18C Type R engines
typicalModels: DC2 Type R / GS-R / LS / RS

engineYears.h2: Honda Integra Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Honda Integra's production timeline (1989-2006), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The DA6 Integra launched Honda's first DOHC VTEC engine in the UK-imported XSi and RSi models.
keyChanges: Introduction of B16A DOHC VTEC engine (158bhp)
XSi and RSi trim levels with lightweight focus
knownFor: Distributor failure common on OBD0/OBD1 units
Timing belt replacement critical at 60k mile intervals
years.cta: Get quotes for 1989-1993 Honda Integra replacement engines
years.ticker: Honda Integra B16A engine replacement • DA6 distributor failure fix • Integra timing belt service cost

years.preview: DC2 Launch: B18B Non-VTEC & B18C VTEC Arrive
years.description: The DC2 chassis introduced the 1.8L B18 series, with non-VTEC B18B in LS/RS and VTEC B18C in GS-R/SiR-G models.
knownFor: B18B offers reliable daily driving with lower insurance
B18C variants feature VTEC engagement and stronger performance
Timing belt service remains critical on all B18 engines
years.cta: Get quotes for 1994-1995 Honda Integra replacement engines
years.ticker: Honda Integra B18B engine cost • DC2 GS-R VTEC solenoid fix • Integra LS engine replacement

years.preview: DC2 Type R Era: B18C Dominates
years.description: The DC2 Integra Type R (1996-2001) became the most sought-after variant, powered by the hand-built B18C VTEC engine.
keyChanges: DC2 Type R launched with B18C (190bhp UKDM / 200ps JDM)
96 spec (4x114.3 studs) and 98 spec (5x114.3 studs) revisions
Limited edition Type Rx (1999) with same B18C
popularModels: Honda Integra DC2 Type R (96 spec / 98 spec)
knownFor: Hand-built B18C with balanced rotating assembly
VTEC solenoid failure common at 40k-80k miles
High-mileage oil consumption from valve seal wear
years.cta: Get quotes for 1996-2001 Honda Integra Type R replacement engines
years.ticker: DC2 Type R B18C engine price • Integra VTEC not engaging fix • B18C oil consumption rebuild cost
enquiries: DC2 Type R B18C engine price

years.preview: DC5 Type R: K20A i-VTEC Era
years.description: The final DC5 Integra Type R (2001-2006) introduced the advanced K20A i-VTEC engine with 217bhp and a 6-speed gearbox.
keyChanges: DC5 chassis replaces DC2; K20A i-VTEC replaces B18C
Introduction of drive-by-wire throttle and advanced VTC system
2005 facelift with K20Z1 variant (updated ECU mapping)
knownFor: Smooth i-VTEC power delivery with 8,000rpm redline
VTC actuator rattle on cold start common at 30k-70k miles
Timing chain (not belt) reduces maintenance vs B-series
years.cta: Get quotes for 2001-2006 Honda Integra DC5 replacement engines
years.ticker: DC5 Type R K20A engine cost • Integra K20A VTC rattle fix • DC5 engine replacement price UK
enquiries: DC5 Type R K20A engine cost

faq.h2: Honda Integra Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Honda Integra engine?
items.answer: Honda Integra engine replacement costs range from £1,200 for a used B18B to £4,500 for a rebuilt K20A. Rebuilt B18C Type R engines typically cost £1,695-£2,500 supply-only.
keyPoints: Used engines start around £950-£1,200 for budget builds
Rebuilt B18C engines: £1,695-£2,500 supply-only
K20A DC5 Type R rebuilt: £2,800-£4,500 supply-only
Supply & fit labour adds £450-£1,200 depending on complexity
items.cta: Compare Honda Integra engine replacement prices now

items.question: What engine is in a DC2 Integra Type R?
items.answer: The DC2 Integra Type R uses the B18C engine: JDM versions produce 200ps (197bhp), while UKDM B18C6 units produce 190bhp with lower compression.
keyPoints: B18C: 1.8L DOHC VTEC, hand-built, 190-200hp
JDM B18C: 11.1:1 compression, 200ps, requires 98 RON fuel
UKDM B18C6: 10.6:1 compression, 190bhp, OBD2
All B18C variants use timing BELT, replace every 60k miles
items.cta: Get quotes for DC2 Integra Type R B18C engine replacement

items.question: Can I put a K20 engine in a DC2 Integra?
items.answer: Yes, K-swapping a K20A or K20A2 into a DC2 Integra is common but requires custom mounts, wiring, and ECU work, with total costs of £4,000-£8,000.
keyPoints: K20A2 from EP3 Civic Type R is a popular swap donor
Requires K-series engine mounts, wiring harness, and ECU
Budget £4,000-£8,000 for full professional K-swap
Retains VTEC/i-VTEC with modern drive-by-wire benefits
items.cta: Enquire about Honda Integra K-swap engine packages

items.question: Are Honda Integra engines reliable?
items.answer: Yes, B18C and K20A engines are robust when maintained, but watch for VTEC solenoid issues, timing belt neglect on B-series, and oil consumption on high-mileage examples.
keyPoints: B-series: Replace timing belt every 60k miles to avoid catastrophic failure
VTEC solenoid failure common at 40k-80k miles on B18C
K20A: VTC actuator rattle on cold start, but timing chain is more reliable
Regular oil changes with 5W-40 synthetic critical for VTEC longevity
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Honda Integra engine options with warranty

items.question: Does the Integra Type R have a timing belt or chain?
items.answer: B18C and B16A engines in DC2/DA6 Type R models use a timing BELT requiring replacement every 60,000 miles. The DC5 K20A uses a timing CHAIN.
keyPoints: B-series (B16A, B18C, B18B): Timing BELT, 60k mile interval
K-series (K20A): Timing CHAIN, tensioner can wear but less frequent service
Belt failure on B-series causes catastrophic valve damage (interference engine)
Always replace water pump and tensioner during belt service
items.cta: Get quotes for Honda Integra timing belt service or engine replacement

items.question: Where can I buy a Honda Integra engine in the UK?
items.answer: Compare prices from vetted UK Honda Integra specialists on this page, sourcing rebuilt, reconditioned and used B18C, B16A and K20A engines with warranty.
keyPoints: EngineMarket connects you with 100+ UK Honda specialists
All engines include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
Supply-only or full supply & fit options available nationwide
Enter your registration to get tailored quotes for your exact variant
items.cta: Get Free Honda Integra Engine Quotes from UK specialists

items.question: Is it worth rebuilding a B18C engine?
items.answer: Yes, rebuilding a B18C is worthwhile for genuine Type R owners, costing £2,000-£3,500 versus £1,695-£2,500 for a pre-rebuilt unit with warranty.
keyPoints: Rebuild costs £2,000-£3,500 if block is in good condition
Pre-rebuilt units from £1,695 include warranty and testing
Upgraded valve seals and piston rings address oil consumption
Ideal for preserving a genuine Type R's value and performance
items.cta: Compare rebuilt B18C engine prices for your Honda Integra

items.question: What is the difference between B18C and B18C6?
items.answer: JDM B18C produces 200ps with 11.1:1 compression; UKDM B18C6 produces 190bhp with 10.6:1 compression and OBD2 emissions equipment.
keyPoints: B18C (JDM): 200ps, 11.1:1 compression, OBD1, requires 98 RON
B18C6 (UKDM): 190bhp, 10.6:1 compression, OBD2, runs on 95 RON
Both share same block architecture and VTEC system
B18C6 is more common and often cheaper for UK replacements
items.cta: Get quotes for B18C or B18C6 Honda Integra engine replacement

items.question: What is a K-swap for Integra?
items.answer: A K-swap replaces the original B-series engine with a K20A or K24 from a later Honda, offering more power, modern electronics and improved reliability.
keyPoints: Popular donors: EP3 Civic Type R (K20A2), DC5 Type R (K20A)
Requires custom mounts, wiring harness, ECU and often gearbox
Total cost £4,000-£8,000 for professional installation
Delivers 200-220hp with i-VTEC and drive-by-wire throttle
items.cta: Enquire about Honda Integra K-swap engine and conversion packages

items.question: What oil should I use in a Honda Integra B18C engine?
items.answer: Use 5W-40 fully synthetic oil for B18C engines to maintain VTEC oil pressure; change every 5,000-7,500 miles for optimal longevity.
keyPoints: B18C requires 5W-40 fully synthetic for VTEC solenoid operation
Change interval: 5,000 miles for track use, 7,500 for road
Use Honda-spec or ACEA A3/B4 rated oils
Monitor oil level monthly; top up if consumption exceeds 1L/1,000 miles
